evidence: risk_model.py contains four nearly identical train_*() functions (train_risk_model, train_tier1_risk_model, train_tier1_v1_risk_model, train_tier1_v1_design_model) that share the same load-split-train-evaluate-metrics structure with only minor variations in feature columns and class-check logic. This is a monolith-by-copy pattern that makes the module hard to maintain.

Similarly, risk_model.py has three nearly identical load_*_dataset() functions (load_failure_dataset, load_tier1_dataset, load_design_dataset, load_tier1_v1_dataset) differing only in which column constants they reference.

The _compute_grid_plus_isls function in hypatia_adapter.py (lines 772-890) has two large blocks for intra-plane and inter-plane links that are structurally identical: check added_links, compute distance, check LOS, check link budget, append ISLLink. The only differences are the partner computation and the link_type tag. This is a clear case where extraction of a helper would reduce duplication and reveal intent.

The run_tier1_rollout function in tier1_rollout.py and the SatNetTemporalDataset.get() method in gnn_dataset.py both independently implement failure application logic (remove failed nodes, then remove failed edges). This is duplicated procedural code for the same semantic operation.

The confusion matrix tracking in train_gnn_model.py evaluate() (lines 226-234) uses four separate if/elif branches to update TP/FP/TN/FN counters. This is verbose but functionally correct; it could be more direct with a lookup or sklearn's confusion_matrix.

visualize_constellation.py main() is a 130-line procedural function that mixes setup, data extraction, plotting, and I/O. The control flow is linear and readable, but the function handles four distinct responsibilities without any extraction.
labels.py and legacy/failures.py demonstrate good low-level elegance: small pure functions with direct control flow, precise edge-case handling (empty graph checks), and no defensive sprawl.

tier1_rollout.py dataclasses (Tier1RolloutConfig, Tier1RolloutStep, etc.) are cleanly structured with well-bounded state. The run_tier1_rollout function has clear sequential phases with comments marking each step.
gnn_model.py SatelliteGNN.forward() is direct and intention-revealing: iterate time steps through GCLSTM, pool, classify. Hidden state reset is clearly commented.
LinkBudgetEngine in hypatia_adapter.py has clean method extraction (optical vs RF budgets) with a well-structured evaluate_link that tries preferred mode then falls back.

issues.summary: Four near-identical train_*() functions in risk_model.py duplicate the split-train-evaluate-metrics pipeline with only column/label variations
evidence: train_risk_model (lines 235-283), train_tier1_risk_model (lines 164-225), train_tier1_v1_risk_model (lines 343-423), and train_tier1_v1_design_model (lines 426-507) share identical structure: load data, check class balance, split, create RandomForest, fit, predict, compute metrics dict with accuracy/roc_auc/confusion_matrix/classification_report/feature_importances
The only meaningful differences are: which feature/label columns to use, whether to add extra metadata keys, and the class-check error message text
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared _train_rf_classifier(X, y, cfg, feature_names) helper that handles split/train/evaluate/metrics. Each public function becomes a thin wrapper that loads data and calls the shared helper, eliminating ~200 lines of duplication.

issues.summary: Three nearly identical load_*_dataset() functions differ only in column constant references
evidence: load_failure_dataset (line 228), load_design_dataset (line 99), and load_tier1_dataset (line 156) each read a CSV and select columns from a different constant list. load_tier1_v1_dataset (line 308) adds a label_column parameter but follows the same pattern.
Each is 3-5 lines: read_csv, select columns, cast label
issues.suggestion: Consolidate into a single load_dataset(csv_path, feature_cols, label_col, label_transform=None) function, or at minimum parameterize the column selection.

issues.summary: _compute_grid_plus_isls has two structurally identical blocks for intra-plane and inter-plane links
evidence: Lines 815-847 (intra-plane) and lines 849-888 (inter-plane) follow identical structure: compute partner index, form link_key, check added_links, compute distance, check LOS, check link budget, append ISLLink with stats tracking
The only differences: partner satellite computation, link_type assignment (with seam_link special case)
issues.suggestion: Extract a _try_add_link(current_sat, partner_sat, link_type, ...) helper that encapsulates the LOS check, link budget evaluation, ISLLink creation, and stats tracking. Call it twice with different partner/type arguments.

issues.summary: Failure application logic (remove failed nodes, then failed edges) is duplicated across tier1_rollout.py and gnn_dataset.py
evidence: tier1_rollout.py lines 328-335: copy graph, remove failed nodes, iterate failed_edges and remove if present
gnn_dataset.py lines 259-264: identical pattern - copy graph, remove failed nodes, iterate failed_edges and remove if present
Both operate on a Tier1FailureRealization's failed_nodes and failed_edges sets
issues.suggestion: Add an apply_to_graph(self, G: nx.Graph) -> nx.Graph method to Tier1FailureRealization that encapsulates this logic. Both call sites become one-liners.

issues.summary: visualize_constellation.py main() is a 130-line monolith mixing setup, data extraction, plotting config, and file I/O
evidence: main() (lines 27-154) handles: adapter initialization, TLE/ISL generation, position extraction, matplotlib figure setup, ISL link drawing loop with dateline logic, satellite scatter plot, legend construction, stats annotation, file save, and console output
No helper functions extracted despite four distinct phases marked by print statements
issues.suggestion: Extract at minimum the ISL drawing loop (lines 91-110) and the legend/stats annotation (lines 116-138) into separate functions. This would make the main function read as a sequence of clear phases.

issues.summary: ISLComputationStats aggregation in calculate_isls uses six manual field additions instead of an __iadd__ or merge method
evidence: Lines 1132-1137 manually add each of six fields: total_stats.total_candidate_links += step_stats.total_candidate_links, total_stats.links_rejected_los += step_stats.links_rejected_los, etc.
This is brittle: adding a new stat field requires remembering to update the aggregation loop
issues.suggestion: Add a merge() or __iadd__ method to ISLComputationStats that aggregates all numeric fields, reducing the six lines to one and preventing future field-addition bugs.

root_causes: Copy-paste evolution: risk_model.py accumulated train/load functions for each new dataset schema version without refactoring shared logic into a common helper

Inline-first development: failure application logic was written inline in tier1_rollout.py first, then copied into gnn_dataset.py when the dataset needed the same behavior, rather than being extracted to the shared dataclass
likely_symptoms: isl_stats_manual_aggregation is a minor symptom of the broader pattern of not adding convenience methods to dataclasses
possible_false_positives: visualize_monolith may be acceptable for a one-off visualization script that is not called by other code
